title: “Broccoli” Turns 10 And Lil Yachty Learns The Artwork Is Messed Up
author: Tom Breihan
summary:
DRAM and Lil Yachty released their euphoric collaboration "Broccoli" on Apr. 6, 2016. Great song! "Broccoli" reached #5 on the Hot 100, and it was a big part of Yachty's rise to the stardom that he still enjoys today. The song officially turned 10 on Monday, which means it's the perfect time for a viral tweet about the single's cover art and its terrible graphic design.

The post “Broccoli” Turns 10 And Lil Yachty Learns The Artwork Is Messed Up appeared first on Stereogum.
full_text:
DRAM and Lil Yachty released their euphoric collaboration "Broccoli" on Apr. 6, 2016. Great song! "Broccoli" reached #5 on the Hot 100, and it was a big part of Yachty's rise to the stardom that he still enjoys today. The song officially turned 10 on Monday, which means it's the perfect time for a viral tweet about the single's cover art and its terrible graphic design.

Lil Yachty himself was caught by surprise.

In other Lil Yachty news, the man is a big wrestling fan who's often on camera in the front row at WWE shows. On last week's episode of Smackdown, WWE wrestler Trick Williams had Yachty in the ring, and he invited Yachty to WrestleMania, where Williams will take on Sami Zayn for the US Championship. Presumably, Yachty won't actually wrestle, but he'll probably get involved. That's what tends to happen with guest celebrities at WrestleMania. 

As Billboard reports, Yachty is now facing down online backlash from wrestling fans who are sick of seeing WWE trot out all the famous people that the company can find, and he doesn't seem too happy about it.

Yachty can probably blame Travis Scott for that.
